search_text: Re-purposing Perceptual Hashing based Client Side Scanning for Physical Surveillance 重新利用基于感知哈希的客户端扫描进行物理监控 Content scanning systems employ perceptual hashing algorithms to scan user content for illegal material, such as child pornography or terrorist recruitment flyers. Perceptual hashing algorithms help determine whether two images are visually similar while preserving the privacy of the input images. Several efforts from industry and academia propose to conduct content scanning on client devices such as smartphones due to the impending roll out of end-to-end encryption that will make server-side content scanning difficult. However, these proposals have met with strong criticism because of the potential for the technology to be misused and re-purposed. Our work informs this conversation by experimentally characterizing the potential for one type of misuse -- attackers manipulating the content scanning system to perform physical surveillance on target locations. Our contributions are threefold: (1) we offer a definition of physical surveillance in the context of client-side image scanning systems; (2) we experimentally characterize this risk and create a surveillance algorithm that achieves physical surveillance rates of >40% by poisoning 5% of the perceptual hash database; (3) we experimentally study the trade-off between the robustness of client-side image scanning systems and surveillance, showing that more robust detection of illegal material leads to increased potential for physical surveillance. 内容扫描系统采用感知哈希算法来扫描用户内容中是否存在非法材料，例如儿童色情内容或恐怖分子招募传单。感知哈希算法有助于确定两个图像在视觉上是否相似，同时保护输入图像的隐私。业界和学术界的一些努力建议在智能手机等客户端设备上进行内容扫描，因为即将推出的端到端加密将使服务器端内容扫描变得困难。然而，这些提议遭到了强烈批评，因为该技术可能被滥用和重新利用。我们的工作通过实验描述一种滥用的可能性，即攻击者操纵内容扫描系统对目标位置进行物理监视，从而为这一对话提供信息。我们的贡献有三个：（1）我们在客户端图像扫描系统的背景下提供了物理监控的定义； (2) 我们通过实验描述了这种风险，并创建了一种监视算法，通过毒害 5% 的感知哈希数据库，实现了 >40% 的物理监视率； (3) 我们通过实验研究了客户端图像扫描系统的鲁棒性与监视之间的权衡，结果表明，对非法材料的更强大的检测会增加物理监视的潜力。 本文研究了基于感知哈希的客户端扫描技术被滥用于物理监控的可能性，并提出了相应的实验分析。 Ashish Hooda,Andrey Labunets,Tadayoshi Kohno,Earlence Fernandes cs.CR,cs.CV

authors_text: Mingrui Zhu,Xiao He,Nannan Wang,Xiaoyu Wang,Xinbo Gao
title: All-to-key Attention for Arbitrary Style Transfer
title_zh: 全键关注任意风格转移
abstract: Attention-based arbitrary style transfer studies have shown promising performance in synthesizing vivid local style details. They typically use the all-to-all attention mechanism -- each position of content features is fully matched to all positions of style features. However, all-to-all attention tends to generate distorted style patterns and has quadratic complexity, limiting the effectiveness and efficiency of arbitrary style transfer. In this paper, we propose a novel all-to-key attention mechanism -- each position of content features is matched to stable key positions of style features -- that is more in line with the characteristics of style transfer. Specifically, it integrates two newly proposed attention forms: distributed and progressive attention. Distributed attention assigns attention to key style representations that depict the style distribution of local regions; Progressive attention pays attention from coarse-grained regions to fine-grained key positions. The resultant module, dubbed StyA2K, shows extraordinary performance in preserving the semantic structure and rendering consistent style patterns. Qualitative and quantitative comparisons with state-of-the-art methods demonstrate the superior performance of our approach.
summary: Attention-based arbitrary style transfer studies have shown promising performance in synthesizing vivid local style details. They typically use the all-to-all attention mechanism -- each position of content features is fully matched to all positions of style features. However, all-to-all attention tends to generate distorted style patterns and has quadratic complexity, limiting the effectiveness and efficiency of arbitrary style transfer. In this paper, we propose a novel all-to-key attention mechanism -- each position of content features is matched to stable key positions of style features -- that is more in line with the characteristics of style transfer. Specifically, it integrates two newly proposed attention forms: distributed and progressive attention. Distributed attention assigns attention to key style representations that depict the style distribution of local regions; Progressive attention pays attention from coarse-grained regions to fine-grained key positions. The resultant module, dubbed StyA2K, shows extraordinary performance in preserving the semantic structure and rendering consistent style patterns. Qualitative and quantitative comparisons with state-of-the-art methods demonstrate the superior performance of our approach.
abstract_zh: 基于注意力的任意风格迁移研究在合成生动的当地风格细节方面表现出了良好的性能。他们通常使用全对所有的注意力机制——内容特征的每个位置都与风格特征的所有位置完全匹配。然而，全面关注往往会产生扭曲的风格模式，并且具有二次复杂性，限制了任意风格迁移的有效性和效率。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的全键注意力机制——内容特征的每个位置都与风格特征的稳定关键位置相匹配——这更符合风格迁移的特征。具体来说，它集成了两种新提出的注意力形式：分布式注意力和渐进式注意力。分布式注意力将注意力分配给描述局部区域风格分布的关键风格表示；渐进注意力关注从粗粒度区域到细粒度关键位置。由此产生的模块被称为 StyA2K，在保留语义结构和呈现一致的风格模式方面表现出了非凡的性能。与最先进方法的定性和定量比较证明了我们方法的卓越性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新的注意力机制（All-to-key Attention）用于任意风格迁移任务，以解决现有方法在风格细节生成和计算效率上的不足。
AI.motivation: 现有基于注意力的任意风格迁移方法采用全连接注意力机制，存在生成风格失真和计算复杂度高的问题，限制了效果与效率。
AI.method: 设计All-to-Key注意力机制，通过分布式注意力捕捉局部风格分布，结合渐进式注意力从粗到细匹配关键位置，构建StyA2K模块。
AI.result: 在定性和定量实验中，StyA2K模块在保留语义结构和生成风格一致性方面优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: All-to-Key注意力机制有效解决了传统方法的局限性，在风格迁移任务中实现了更优的性能与效率平衡。

authors_text: Hamidreza Mahini,Hamid Mousavi,Masoud Daneshtalab
title: GTFLAT: Game Theory Based Add-On For Empowering Federated Learning Aggregation Techniques
title_zh: GTFLAT：基于博弈论的附加组件，用于增强联邦学习聚合技术
abstract: GTFLAT, as a game theory-based add-on, addresses an important research question: How can a federated learning algorithm achieve better performance and training efficiency by setting more effective adaptive weights for averaging in the model aggregation phase? The main objectives for the ideal method of answering the question are: (1) empowering federated learning algorithms to reach better performance in fewer communication rounds, notably in the face of heterogeneous scenarios, and last but not least, (2) being easy to use alongside the state-of-the-art federated learning algorithms as a new module. To this end, GTFLAT models the averaging task as a strategic game among active users. Then it proposes a systematic solution based on the population game and evolutionary dynamics to find the equilibrium. In contrast with existing approaches that impose the weights on the participants, GTFLAT concludes a self-enforcement agreement among clients in a way that none of them is motivated to deviate from it individually. The results reveal that, on average, using GTFLAT increases the top-1 test accuracy by 1.38%, while it needs 21.06% fewer communication rounds to reach the accuracy.
summary: GTFLAT, as a game theory-based add-on, addresses an important research question: How can a federated learning algorithm achieve better performance and training efficiency by setting more effective adaptive weights for averaging in the model aggregation phase? The main objectives for the ideal method of answering the question are: (1) empowering federated learning algorithms to reach better performance in fewer communication rounds, notably in the face of heterogeneous scenarios, and last but not least, (2) being easy to use alongside the state-of-the-art federated learning algorithms as a new module. To this end, GTFLAT models the averaging task as a strategic game among active users. Then it proposes a systematic solution based on the population game and evolutionary dynamics to find the equilibrium. In contrast with existing approaches that impose the weights on the participants, GTFLAT concludes a self-enforcement agreement among clients in a way that none of them is motivated to deviate from it individually. The results reveal that, on average, using GTFLAT increases the top-1 test accuracy by 1.38%, while it needs 21.06% fewer communication rounds to reach the accuracy.
abstract_zh: GTFLAT 作为一个基于博弈论的插件，解决了一个重要的研究问题：联邦学习算法如何通过在模型聚合阶段设置更有效的平均自适应权重来获得更好的性能和训练效率？回答这个问题的理想方法的主要目标是：(1) 使联邦学习算法能够在更少的通信轮次中达到更好的性能，特别是在面对异构场景时，最后但并非最不重要的一点是，(2) 作为一个新模块，易于与最先进的联邦学习算法一起使用。为此，GTFLAT 将平均任务建模为活跃用户之间的策略游戏。然后提出基于群体博弈和进化动力学的系统解决方案来寻找均衡。与向参与者施加权重的现有方法相比，GTFLAT 在客户之间达成自我执行协议，其中任何人都没有动机单独偏离该协议。结果表明，平均而言，使用 GTFLAT 将 top-1 测试准确率提高了 1.38%，同时达到准确率所需的通信轮数减少了 21.06%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于博弈论的附加模块GTFLAT，用于提升联邦学习中的模型聚合效果。
AI.motivation: 联邦学习在模型聚合阶段需要设置有效的自适应权重以提高性能和训练效率，尤其是在异构场景下。
AI.method: GTFLAT将模型聚合任务建模为用户之间的战略博弈，并基于群体博弈和进化动力学提出系统性解决方案，以达成均衡状态。
AI.result: 使用GTFLAT平均提升了1.38%的top-1测试准确率，并减少了21.06%的通信轮次以达到该准确率。
AI.conclusion: GTFLAT作为一种易于集成的模块，能够有效提升联邦学习的性能和效率，适用于各种异构场景。

search_text: GTFLAT: Game Theory Based Add-On For Empowering Federated Learning Aggregation Techniques GTFLAT：基于博弈论的附加组件，用于增强联邦学习聚合技术 GTFLAT, as a game theory-based add-on, addresses an important research question: How can a federated learning algorithm achieve better performance and training efficiency by setting more effective adaptive weights for averaging in the model aggregation phase? The main objectives for the ideal method of answering the question are: (1) empowering federated learning algorithms to reach better performance in fewer communication rounds, notably in the face of heterogeneous scenarios, and last but not least, (2) being easy to use alongside the state-of-the-art federated learning algorithms as a new module. To this end, GTFLAT models the averaging task as a strategic game among active users. Then it proposes a systematic solution based on the population game and evolutionary dynamics to find the equilibrium. In contrast with existing approaches that impose the weights on the participants, GTFLAT concludes a self-enforcement agreement among clients in a way that none of them is motivated to deviate from it individually. The results reveal that, on average, using GTFLAT increases the top-1 test accuracy by 1.38%, while it needs 21.06% fewer communication rounds to reach the accuracy. GTFLAT 作为一个基于博弈论的插件，解决了一个重要的研究问题：联邦学习算法如何通过在模型聚合阶段设置更有效的平均自适应权重来获得更好的性能和训练效率？回答这个问题的理想方法的主要目标是：(1) 使联邦学习算法能够在更少的通信轮次中达到更好的性能，特别是在面对异构场景时，最后但并非最不重要的一点是，(2) 作为一个新模块，易于与最先进的联邦学习算法一起使用。为此，GTFLAT 将平均任务建模为活跃用户之间的策略游戏。然后提出基于群体博弈和进化动力学的系统解决方案来寻找均衡。与向参与者施加权重的现有方法相比，GTFLAT 在客户之间达成自我执行协议，其中任何人都没有动机单独偏离该协议。结果表明，平均而言，使用 GTFLAT 将 top-1 测试准确率提高了 1.38%，同时达到准确率所需的通信轮数减少了 21.06%。 本文提出了一种基于博弈论的附加模块GTFLAT，用于提升联邦学习中的模型聚合效果。 Hamidreza Mahini,Hamid Mousavi,Masoud Daneshtalab cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.DC,cs.GT

authors_text: Xiaoshui Huang,Zhou Huang,Sheng Li,Wentao Qu,Tong He,Yuenan Hou,Yifan Zuo,Wanli Ouyang
title: EPCL: Frozen CLIP Transformer is An Efficient Point Cloud Encoder
title_zh: EPCL：Frozen CLIP Transformer 是一种高效的点云编码器
abstract: The pretrain-finetune paradigm has achieved great success in NLP and 2D image fields because of the high-quality representation ability and transferability of their pretrained models. However, pretraining such a strong model is difficult in the 3D point cloud field due to the limited amount of point cloud sequences. This paper introduces \textbf{E}fficient \textbf{P}oint \textbf{C}loud \textbf{L}earning (EPCL), an effective and efficient point cloud learner for directly training high-quality point cloud models with a frozen CLIP transformer. Our EPCL connects the 2D and 3D modalities by semantically aligning the image features and point cloud features without paired 2D-3D data. Specifically, the input point cloud is divided into a series of local patches, which are converted to token embeddings by the designed point cloud tokenizer. These token embeddings are concatenated with a task token and fed into the frozen CLIP transformer to learn point cloud representation. The intuition is that the proposed point cloud tokenizer projects the input point cloud into a unified token space that is similar to the 2D images. Comprehensive experiments on 3D detection, semantic segmentation, classification and few-shot learning demonstrate that the CLIP transformer can serve as an efficient point cloud encoder and our method achieves promising performance on both indoor and outdoor benchmarks. In particular, performance gains brought by our EPCL are $\textbf{19.7}$ AP$_{50}$ on ScanNet V2 detection, $\textbf{4.4}$ mIoU on S3DIS segmentation and $\textbf{1.2}$ mIoU on SemanticKITTI segmentation compared to contemporary pretrained models. Code is available at \url{https://github.com/XiaoshuiHuang/EPCL}.
summary: The pretrain-finetune paradigm has achieved great success in NLP and 2D image fields because of the high-quality representation ability and transferability of their pretrained models. However, pretraining such a strong model is difficult in the 3D point cloud field due to the limited amount of point cloud sequences. This paper introduces \textbf{E}fficient \textbf{P}oint \textbf{C}loud \textbf{L}earning (EPCL), an effective and efficient point cloud learner for directly training high-quality point cloud models with a frozen CLIP transformer. Our EPCL connects the 2D and 3D modalities by semantically aligning the image features and point cloud features without paired 2D-3D data. Specifically, the input point cloud is divided into a series of local patches, which are converted to token embeddings by the designed point cloud tokenizer. These token embeddings are concatenated with a task token and fed into the frozen CLIP transformer to learn point cloud representation. The intuition is that the proposed point cloud tokenizer projects the input point cloud into a unified token space that is similar to the 2D images. Comprehensive experiments on 3D detection, semantic segmentation, classification and few-shot learning demonstrate that the CLIP transformer can serve as an efficient point cloud encoder and our method achieves promising performance on both indoor and outdoor benchmarks. In particular, performance gains brought by our EPCL are $\textbf{19.7}$ AP$_{50}$ on ScanNet V2 detection, $\textbf{4.4}$ mIoU on S3DIS segmentation and $\textbf{1.2}$ mIoU on SemanticKITTI segmentation compared to contemporary pretrained models. Code is available at \url{https://github.com/XiaoshuiHuang/EPCL}.
abstract_zh: pretrain-finetune 范式因其预训练模型的高质量表示能力和可迁移性而在 NLP 和 2D 图像领域取得了巨大成功。然而，由于点云序列数量有限，在 3D 点云领域预训练如此强大的模型是很困难的。本文介绍了 \textbf{E}fficient \textbf{P}oint \textbf{C}loud \textbf{L}earning (EPCL)，这是一种有效且高效的点云学习器，用于使用冻结的 CLIP 转换器直接训练高质量点云模型。我们的 EPCL 通过在语义上对齐图像特征和点云特征来连接 2D 和 3D 模态，而无需配对 2D-3D 数据。具体来说，输入点云被分为一系列局部补丁，由设计的点云标记器将其转换为标记嵌入。这些令牌嵌入与任务令牌连接并输入到冻结的 CLIP 转换器中以学习点云表示。直觉是，所提出的点云标记器将输入点云投影到类似于 2D 图像的统一标记空间中。 3D 检测、语义分割、分类和少样本学习的综合实验表明，CLIP 变换器可以作为高效的点云编码器，并且我们的方法在室内和室外基准测试中都取得了良好的性能。特别是，与当代预训练模型相比，我们的 EPCL 在 ScanNet V2 检测上带来的性能提升为 $\textbf{19.7}$ AP$_{50}$，在 S3DIS 分割上带来 $\textbf{4.4}$ mIoU，在 SemanticKITTI 分割上带来 $\textbf{1.2}$ mIoU。代码可在 \url{https://github.com/XiaoshuiHuang/EPCL} 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出EPCL方法，利用冻结的CLIP Transformer作为高效的点云编码器，无需配对的2D-3D数据即可实现点云特征学习。
AI.motivation: 3D点云领域因数据量有限难以训练强预训练模型，而CLIP等2D模型在跨模态对齐方面表现优异，因此需探索无需配对数据的跨模态点云学习方法。
AI.method: 将点云划分为局部块并通过设计的点云分词器转换为token嵌入，与任务token拼接后输入冻结的CLIP Transformer，通过语义对齐实现点云表征学习。
AI.result: 在ScanNet V2、S3DIS和SemanticKITTI等基准测试中，EPCL分别取得19.7 AP50、4.4 mIoU和1.2 mIoU的性能提升，优于现有预训练模型。
AI.conclusion: 冻结CLIP Transformer可作为高效的点云编码器，EPCL通过跨模态语义对齐有效解决了点云领域数据稀缺问题，验证了方法的普适性与有效性。

authors_text: Yixiong Chen,Chunhui Zhang,Chris H. Q. Ding,Li Liu
title: Generating and Weighting Semantically Consistent Sample Pairs for Ultrasound Contrastive Learning
title_zh: 为超声对比学习生成和加权语义一致的样本对
abstract: Well-annotated medical datasets enable deep neural networks (DNNs) to gain strong power in extracting lesion-related features. Building such large and well-designed medical datasets is costly due to the need for high-level expertise. Model pre-training based on ImageNet is a common practice to gain better generalization when the data amount is limited. However, it suffers from the domain gap between natural and medical images. In this work, we pre-train DNNs on ultrasound (US) domains instead of ImageNet to reduce the domain gap in medical US applications. To learn US image representations based on unlabeled US videos, we propose a novel meta-learning-based contrastive learning method, namely Meta Ultrasound Contrastive Learning (Meta-USCL). To tackle the key challenge of obtaining semantically consistent sample pairs for contrastive learning, we present a positive pair generation module along with an automatic sample weighting module based on meta-learning. Experimental results on multiple computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) problems, including pneumonia detection, breast cancer classification, and breast tumor segmentation, show that the proposed self-supervised method reaches state-of-the-art (SOTA). The codes are available at https://github.com/Schuture/Meta-USCL.
summary: Well-annotated medical datasets enable deep neural networks (DNNs) to gain strong power in extracting lesion-related features. Building such large and well-designed medical datasets is costly due to the need for high-level expertise. Model pre-training based on ImageNet is a common practice to gain better generalization when the data amount is limited. However, it suffers from the domain gap between natural and medical images. In this work, we pre-train DNNs on ultrasound (US) domains instead of ImageNet to reduce the domain gap in medical US applications. To learn US image representations based on unlabeled US videos, we propose a novel meta-learning-based contrastive learning method, namely Meta Ultrasound Contrastive Learning (Meta-USCL). To tackle the key challenge of obtaining semantically consistent sample pairs for contrastive learning, we present a positive pair generation module along with an automatic sample weighting module based on meta-learning. Experimental results on multiple computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) problems, including pneumonia detection, breast cancer classification, and breast tumor segmentation, show that the proposed self-supervised method reaches state-of-the-art (SOTA). The codes are available at https://github.com/Schuture/Meta-USCL.
abstract_zh: 注释良好的医学数据集使深度神经网络（DNN）能够在提取病变相关特征方面获得强大的能力。由于需要高水平的专业知识，构建如此庞大且设计良好的医疗数据集成本高昂。基于ImageNet的模型预训练是在数据量有限的情况下获得更好泛化能力的常见做法。然而，它受到自然图像和医学图像之间的域差距的影响。在这项工作中，我们在超声（US）域而不是 ImageNet 上预训练 DNN，以缩小美国医学应用中的域差距。为了根据未标记的美国视频学习美国图像表示，我们提出了一种新颖的基于元学习的对比学习方法，即元超声对比学习（Meta-USCL）。为了解决获得语义一致的样本对以进行对比学习的关键挑战，我们提出了一个正对生成模块以及一个基于元学习的自动样本加权模块。在肺炎检测、乳腺癌分类和乳腺肿瘤分割等多个计算机辅助诊断（CAD）问题上的实验结果表明，所提出的自监督方法达到了最先进的（SOTA）。这些代码可在 https://github.com/Schuture/Meta-USCL 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于元学习的对比学习方法Meta-USCL，用于在未标注的超声视频上学习语义一致的样本对，以提升医学图像分析任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 医学数据集标注成本高，且基于ImageNet的预训练模型在医学图像上存在领域差距，因此需要一种针对超声图像的自监督学习方法。
AI.method: 提出了一种基于元学习的对比学习方法Meta-USCL，包含正样本对生成模块和基于元学习的自动样本加权模块，以解决语义一致样本对获取的挑战。
AI.result: 在肺炎检测、乳腺癌分类和乳腺肿瘤分割等任务上，该自监督方法达到了最先进的性能。
AI.conclusion: Meta-USCL方法有效减少了医学超声图像与自然图像之间的领域差距，提高了模型在医学任务中的表现。

To tackle the key challenge of obtaining semantically consistent sample pairs for contrastive learning, we present a positive pair generation module along with an automatic sample weighting module based on meta-learning. Experimental results on multiple computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) problems, including pneumonia detection, breast cancer classification, and breast tumor segmentation, show that the proposed self-supervised method reaches state-of-the-art (SOTA). The codes are available at https://github.com/Schuture/Meta-USCL. 注释良好的医学数据集使深度神经网络（DNN）能够在提取病变相关特征方面获得强大的能力。由于需要高水平的专业知识，构建如此庞大且设计良好的医疗数据集成本高昂。基于ImageNet的模型预训练是在数据量有限的情况下获得更好泛化能力的常见做法。然而，它受到自然图像和医学图像之间的域差距的影响。在这项工作中，我们在超声（US）域而不是 ImageNet 上预训练 DNN，以缩小美国医学应用中的域差距。为了根据未标记的美国视频学习美国图像表示，我们提出了一种新颖的基于元学习的对比学习方法，即元超声对比学习（Meta-USCL）。为了解决获得语义一致的样本对以进行对比学习的关键挑战，我们提出了一个正对生成模块以及一个基于元学习的自动样本加权模块。在肺炎检测、乳腺癌分类和乳腺肿瘤分割等多个计算机辅助诊断（CAD）问题上的实验结果表明，所提出的自监督方法达到了最先进的（SOTA）。这些代码可在 https://github.com/Schuture/Meta-USCL 获取。 本文提出了一种基于元学习的对比学习方法Meta-USCL，用于在未标注的超声视频上学习语义一致的样本对，以提升医学图像分析任务的性能。 Yixiong Chen,Chunhui Zhang,Chris H. Q. Ding,Li Liu cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Zhen Wang,Shijie Zhou,Jeong Joon Park,Despoina Paschalidou,Suya You,Gordon Wetzstein,Leonidas Guibas,Achuta Kadambi
title: ALTO: Alternating Latent Topologies for Implicit 3D Reconstruction
title_zh: ALTO：用于隐式 3D 重建的交替潜在拓扑
abstract: This work introduces alternating latent topologies (ALTO) for high-fidelity reconstruction of implicit 3D surfaces from noisy point clouds. Previous work identifies that the spatial arrangement of latent encodings is important to recover detail. One school of thought is to encode a latent vector for each point (point latents). Another school of thought is to project point latents into a grid (grid latents) which could be a voxel grid or triplane grid. Each school of thought has tradeoffs. Grid latents are coarse and lose high-frequency detail. In contrast, point latents preserve detail. However, point latents are more difficult to decode into a surface, and quality and runtime suffer. In this paper, we propose ALTO to sequentially alternate between geometric representations, before converging to an easy-to-decode latent. We find that this preserves spatial expressiveness and makes decoding lightweight. We validate ALTO on implicit 3D recovery and observe not only a performance improvement over the state-of-the-art, but a runtime improvement of 3-10$\times$. Project website at https://visual.ee.ucla.edu/alto.htm/.
summary: This work introduces alternating latent topologies (ALTO) for high-fidelity reconstruction of implicit 3D surfaces from noisy point clouds. Previous work identifies that the spatial arrangement of latent encodings is important to recover detail. One school of thought is to encode a latent vector for each point (point latents). Another school of thought is to project point latents into a grid (grid latents) which could be a voxel grid or triplane grid. Each school of thought has tradeoffs. Grid latents are coarse and lose high-frequency detail. In contrast, point latents preserve detail. However, point latents are more difficult to decode into a surface, and quality and runtime suffer. In this paper, we propose ALTO to sequentially alternate between geometric representations, before converging to an easy-to-decode latent. We find that this preserves spatial expressiveness and makes decoding lightweight. We validate ALTO on implicit 3D recovery and observe not only a performance improvement over the state-of-the-art, but a runtime improvement of 3-10$\times$. Project website at https://visual.ee.ucla.edu/alto.htm/.
abstract_zh: 这项工作引入了交替潜在拓扑 (ALTO)，用于从噪声点云中对隐式 3D 表面进行高保真重建。先前的工作表明，潜在编码的空间排列对于恢复细节非常重要。一种思想流派是为每个点（潜在点）编码一个潜在向量。另一种思想流派是将潜在点投影到网格（网格潜在）中，该网格可以是体素网格或三平面网格。每种思想流派都有权衡。网格潜伏很粗糙并且丢失了高频细节。相反，潜在点保留了细节。然而，点潜伏更难以解码为表面，并且质量和运行时间受到影响。在本文中，我们建议 ALTO 在收敛到易于解码的潜在模型之前在几何表示之间顺序交替。我们发现这保留了空间表现力并使解码变得轻量级。我们在隐式 3D 恢复上验证 ALTO，不仅观察到相对于最先进技术的性能改进，而且还观察到运行时间改进了 3-10$\times$。项目网站：https://visual.ee.ucla.edu/alto.htm/。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ALTO方法，通过交替使用不同潜在拓扑结构，从噪声点云中高保真重建隐式3D表面。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在网格潜在编码（保留高频细节但解码困难）与点潜在编码（解码效率低）之间存在权衡，需寻找兼顾细节与解码效率的解决方案。
AI.method: ALTO通过顺序交替几何表示形式，逐步收敛到易于解码的潜在表示，保留空间表达能力并降低解码计算量。
AI.result: 在隐式3D重建任务中，ALTO相较现有方法性能提升且运行时间缩短3-10倍。
AI.conclusion: ALTO有效平衡了细节保留与解码效率，为噪声点云的高质量3D重建提供了新思路。

authors_text: Chen Zhang,Guorong Li,Yuankai Qi,Shuhui Wang,Laiyun Qing,Qingming Huang,Ming-Hsuan Yang
title: Exploiting Completeness and Uncertainty of Pseudo Labels for Weakly Supervised Video Anomaly Detection
title_zh: 利用伪标签的完整性和不确定性进行弱监督视频异常检测
abstract: Weakly supervised video anomaly detection aims to identify abnormal events in videos using only video-level labels. Recently, two-stage self-training methods have achieved significant improvements by self-generating pseudo labels and self-refining anomaly scores with these labels. As the pseudo labels play a crucial role, we propose an enhancement framework by exploiting completeness and uncertainty properties for effective self-training. Specifically, we first design a multi-head classification module (each head serves as a classifier) with a diversity loss to maximize the distribution differences of predicted pseudo labels across heads. This encourages the generated pseudo labels to cover as many abnormal events as possible. We then devise an iterative uncertainty pseudo label refinement strategy, which improves not only the initial pseudo labels but also the updated ones obtained by the desired classifier in the second stage. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the proposed method performs favorably against state-of-the-art approaches on the UCF-Crime, TAD, and XD-Violence benchmark datasets.
summary: Weakly supervised video anomaly detection aims to identify abnormal events in videos using only video-level labels. Recently, two-stage self-training methods have achieved significant improvements by self-generating pseudo labels and self-refining anomaly scores with these labels. As the pseudo labels play a crucial role, we propose an enhancement framework by exploiting completeness and uncertainty properties for effective self-training. Specifically, we first design a multi-head classification module (each head serves as a classifier) with a diversity loss to maximize the distribution differences of predicted pseudo labels across heads. This encourages the generated pseudo labels to cover as many abnormal events as possible. We then devise an iterative uncertainty pseudo label refinement strategy, which improves not only the initial pseudo labels but also the updated ones obtained by the desired classifier in the second stage. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the proposed method performs favorably against state-of-the-art approaches on the UCF-Crime, TAD, and XD-Violence benchmark datasets.
abstract_zh: 弱监督视频异常检测旨在仅使用视频级标签来识别视频中的异常事件。最近，两阶段自我训练方法通过自我生成伪标签和使用这些标签自我细化异常分数取得了显着的改进。由于伪标签起着至关重要的作用，我们提出了一个增强框架，通过利用完整性和不确定性属性来进行有效的自我训练。具体来说，我们首先设计一个具有多样性损失的多头分类模块（每个头充当分类器），以最大化各个头之间预测伪标签的分布差异。这鼓励生成的伪标签尽可能多地覆盖异常事件。然后，我们设计了一种迭代不确定性伪标签细化策略，该策略不仅改进了初始伪标签，还改进了第二阶段所需分类器获得的更新伪标签。大量的实验结果表明，所提出的方法在 UCF-Crime、TAD 和 XD-Violence 基准数据集上的性能优于最先进的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种利用伪标签完整性和不确定性的框架，用于弱监督视频异常检测。
AI.motivation: 现有两阶段自训练方法依赖伪标签，但未充分挖掘其完整性和不确定性，导致异常事件覆盖不足和标签可靠性问题。
AI.method: 设计多头分类模块配合多样性损失，最大化伪标签分布差异以提升覆盖率；提出迭代不确定性伪标签优化策略，分阶段提升标签质量。
AI.result: 在UCF-Crime、TAD和XD-Violence数据集上优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过结合伪标签的完整性和不确定性，所提框架有效提升了弱监督视频异常检测的性能。

authors_text: Gabriel Ilharco,Marco Tulio Ribeiro,Mitchell Wortsman,Suchin Gururangan,Ludwig Schmidt,Hannaneh Hajishirzi,Ali Farhadi
title: Editing Models with Task Arithmetic
title_zh: 使用任务算术编辑模型
comment: In Proceedings of the 11th International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR 2023)
comments: In Proceedings of the 11th International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR 2023)
abstract: Changing how pre-trained models behave -- e.g., improving their performance on a downstream task or mitigating biases learned during pre-training -- is a common practice when developing machine learning systems. In this work, we propose a new paradigm for steering the behavior of neural networks, centered around \textit{task vectors}. A task vector specifies a direction in the weight space of a pre-trained model, such that movement in that direction improves performance on the task. We build task vectors by subtracting the weights of a pre-trained model from the weights of the same model after fine-tuning on a task. We show that these task vectors can be modified and combined together through arithmetic operations such as negation and addition, and the behavior of the resulting model is steered accordingly. Negating a task vector decreases performance on the target task, with little change in model behavior on control tasks. Moreover, adding task vectors together can improve performance on multiple tasks at once. Finally, when tasks are linked by an analogy relationship of the form ``A is to B as C is to D", combining task vectors from three of the tasks can improve performance on the fourth, even when no data from the fourth task is used for training. Overall, our experiments with several models, modalities and tasks show that task arithmetic is a simple, efficient and effective way of editing models.
summary: Changing how pre-trained models behave -- e.g., improving their performance on a downstream task or mitigating biases learned during pre-training -- is a common practice when developing machine learning systems. In this work, we propose a new paradigm for steering the behavior of neural networks, centered around \textit{task vectors}. A task vector specifies a direction in the weight space of a pre-trained model, such that movement in that direction improves performance on the task. We build task vectors by subtracting the weights of a pre-trained model from the weights of the same model after fine-tuning on a task. We show that these task vectors can be modified and combined together through arithmetic operations such as negation and addition, and the behavior of the resulting model is steered accordingly. Negating a task vector decreases performance on the target task, with little change in model behavior on control tasks. Moreover, adding task vectors together can improve performance on multiple tasks at once. Finally, when tasks are linked by an analogy relationship of the form ``A is to B as C is to D", combining task vectors from three of the tasks can improve performance on the fourth, even when no data from the fourth task is used for training. Overall, our experiments with several models, modalities and tasks show that task arithmetic is a simple, efficient and effective way of editing models.
abstract_zh: 改变预训练模型的行为方式——例如，提高其在下游任务中的性能或减轻预训练期间学到的偏差——是开发机器学习系统时的常见做法。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新的范式来控制神经网络的行为，以 \textit{任务向量} 为中心。任务向量指定预训练模型的权重空间中的方向，以便沿该方向的移动可以提高任务的性能。在对任务进行微调后，我们通过从同一模型的权重中减去预训练模型的权重来构建任务向量。我们表明，可以通过求反和加法等算术运算来修改和组合这些任务向量，并相应地控制所得模型的行为。否定任务向量会降低目标任务的性能，而控制任务的模型行为几乎没有变化。此外，将任务向量添加在一起可以同时提高多个任务的性能。最后，当任务通过“A 与 B 如同 C 与 D”形式的类比关系链接时，即使第四个任务没有数据用于训练，组合来自三个任务的任务向量也可以提高第四个任务的性能。总的来说，我们对多个模型、模态和任务的实验表明，任务算术是一种简单、高效且有效的模型编辑方式。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于任务向量算术的模型编辑方法，通过加减操作调整预训练模型的行为以提升性能或消除偏见。
AI.motivation: 传统微调方法需重新训练模型才能调整行为，效率低下。本文旨在探索通过简单向量运算直接编辑模型权重空间方向的高效方案。
AI.method: 构建任务向量（微调后权重减去原始权重），通过向量加减运算组合不同任务的方向，无需额外训练即可修改模型行为。
AI.result: 实验表明：否定向量降低目标任务性能；向量相加可同时提升多任务表现；类比关系三任务向量组合能提升第四任务性能（无需该任务数据）。
AI.conclusion: 任务算术提供了一种简单高效的模型编辑范式，在多种模型和任务中均能有效调整模型行为，具有广泛适用性。

search_text: Editing Models with Task Arithmetic 使用任务算术编辑模型 Changing how pre-trained models behave -- e.g., improving their performance on a downstream task or mitigating biases learned during pre-training -- is a common practice when developing machine learning systems. In this work, we propose a new paradigm for steering the behavior of neural networks, centered around \textit{task vectors}. A task vector specifies a direction in the weight space of a pre-trained model, such that movement in that direction improves performance on the task. We build task vectors by subtracting the weights of a pre-trained model from the weights of the same model after fine-tuning on a task. We show that these task vectors can be modified and combined together through arithmetic operations such as negation and addition, and the behavior of the resulting model is steered accordingly. Negating a task vector decreases performance on the target task, with little change in model behavior on control tasks. Moreover, adding task vectors together can improve performance on multiple tasks at once. Finally, when tasks are linked by an analogy relationship of the form ``A is to B as C is to D", combining task vectors from three of the tasks can improve performance on the fourth, even when no data from the fourth task is used for training. Overall, our experiments with several models, modalities and tasks show that task arithmetic is a simple, efficient and effective way of editing models. 改变预训练模型的行为方式——例如，提高其在下游任务中的性能或减轻预训练期间学到的偏差——是开发机器学习系统时的常见做法。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新的范式来控制神经网络的行为，以 \textit{任务向量} 为中心。任务向量指定预训练模型的权重空间中的方向，以便沿该方向的移动可以提高任务的性能。在对任务进行微调后，我们通过从同一模型的权重中减去预训练模型的权重来构建任务向量。我们表明，可以通过求反和加法等算术运算来修改和组合这些任务向量，并相应地控制所得模型的行为。否定任务向量会降低目标任务的性能，而控制任务的模型行为几乎没有变化。此外，将任务向量添加在一起可以同时提高多个任务的性能。最后，当任务通过“A 与 B 如同 C 与 D”形式的类比关系链接时，即使第四个任务没有数据用于训练，组合来自三个任务的任务向量也可以提高第四个任务的性能。总的来说，我们对多个模型、模态和任务的实验表明，任务算术是一种简单、高效且有效的模型编辑方式。 本文提出一种基于任务向量算术的模型编辑方法，通过加减操作调整预训练模型的行为以提升性能或消除偏见。 Gabriel Ilharco,Marco Tulio Ribeiro,Mitchell Wortsman,Suchin Gururangan,Ludwig Schmidt,Hannaneh Hajishirzi,Ali Farhadi cs.LG,cs.CL,cs.CV

authors_text: Chan Hee Song,Jiaman Wu,Clayton Washington,Brian M. Sadler,Wei-Lun Chao,Yu Su
title: LLM-Planner: Few-Shot Grounded Planning for Embodied Agents with Large Language Models
title_zh: LLM-Planner：针对具有大型语言模型的具体代理的少样本扎根规划
abstract: This study focuses on using large language models (LLMs) as a planner for embodied agents that can follow natural language instructions to complete complex tasks in a visually-perceived environment. The high data cost and poor sample efficiency of existing methods hinders the development of versatile agents that are capable of many tasks and can learn new tasks quickly. In this work, we propose a novel method, LLM-Planner, that harnesses the power of large language models to do few-shot planning for embodied agents. We further propose a simple but effective way to enhance LLMs with physical grounding to generate and update plans that are grounded in the current environment. Experiments on the ALFRED dataset show that our method can achieve very competitive few-shot performance: Despite using less than 0.5% of paired training data, LLM-Planner achieves competitive performance with recent baselines that are trained using the full training data. Existing methods can barely complete any task successfully under the same few-shot setting. Our work opens the door for developing versatile and sample-efficient embodied agents that can quickly learn many tasks. Website: https://dki-lab.github.io/LLM-Planner
summary: This study focuses on using large language models (LLMs) as a planner for embodied agents that can follow natural language instructions to complete complex tasks in a visually-perceived environment. The high data cost and poor sample efficiency of existing methods hinders the development of versatile agents that are capable of many tasks and can learn new tasks quickly. In this work, we propose a novel method, LLM-Planner, that harnesses the power of large language models to do few-shot planning for embodied agents. We further propose a simple but effective way to enhance LLMs with physical grounding to generate and update plans that are grounded in the current environment. Experiments on the ALFRED dataset show that our method can achieve very competitive few-shot performance: Despite using less than 0.5% of paired training data, LLM-Planner achieves competitive performance with recent baselines that are trained using the full training data. Existing methods can barely complete any task successfully under the same few-shot setting. Our work opens the door for developing versatile and sample-efficient embodied agents that can quickly learn many tasks. Website: https://dki-lab.github.io/LLM-Planner
abstract_zh: 本研究的重点是使用大型语言模型（LLM）作为实体代理的规划器，实体代理可以遵循自然语言指令在视觉感知环境中完成复杂的任务。现有方法的高数据成本和低样本效率阻碍了能够执行多种任务并能够快速学习新任务的多功能代理的开发。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的方法，LLM-Planner，它利用大型语言模型的力量来为具体代理进行几次规划。我们进一步提出了一种简单但有效的方法来增强法学硕士的物理基础，以生成和更新基于当前环境的计划。 ALFRED 数据集上的实验表明，我们的方法可以实现非常有竞争力的小样本性能：尽管使用了不到 0.5% 的配对训练数据，LLM-Planner 通过使用完整训练数据训练的最新基线实现了有竞争力的性能。现有方法在相同的少样本设置下几乎无法成功完成任何任务。我们的工作为开发多功能且样本高效的实体代理打开了大门，这些代理可以快速学习许多任务。网站：https://dki-lab.github.io/LLM-Planner

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为LLM-Planner的新方法，利用大语言模型（LLMs）作为具身代理的规划器，使其能够通过自然语言指令完成复杂任务。
AI.motivation: 现有的方法在数据成本和样本效率方面存在不足，限制了多功能代理的发展，这些代理需要能够快速学习新任务。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种新颖的方法，利用大语言模型进行少量样本规划，并通过物理接地增强LLMs，以生成和更新与当前环境相关的计划。
AI.result: 在ALFRED数据集上的实验表明，LLM-Planner在使用不到0.5%的配对训练数据的情况下，能够达到与使用全部训练数据的最新基线相当的性能。
AI.conclusion: 本文的工作为开发多功能且样本高效的具身代理铺平了道路，这些代理能够快速学习许多任务。

authors_text: Yijie Lin,Mouxing Yang,Jun Yu,Peng Hu,Changqing Zhang,Xi Peng
title: Graph Matching with Bi-level Noisy Correspondence
title_zh: 具有双层噪声对应的图匹配
abstract: In this paper, we study a novel and widely existing problem in graph matching (GM), namely, Bi-level Noisy Correspondence (BNC), which refers to node-level noisy correspondence (NNC) and edge-level noisy correspondence (ENC). In brief, on the one hand, due to the poor recognizability and viewpoint differences between images, it is inevitable to inaccurately annotate some keypoints with offset and confusion, leading to the mismatch between two associated nodes, i.e., NNC. On the other hand, the noisy node-to-node correspondence will further contaminate the edge-to-edge correspondence, thus leading to ENC. For the BNC challenge, we propose a novel method termed Contrastive Matching with Momentum Distillation. Specifically, the proposed method is with a robust quadratic contrastive loss which enjoys the following merits: i) better exploring the node-to-node and edge-to-edge correlations through a GM customized quadratic contrastive learning paradigm; ii) adaptively penalizing the noisy assignments based on the confidence estimated by the momentum teacher. Extensive experiments on three real-world datasets show the robustness of our model compared with 12 competitive baselines. The code is available at https://github.com/XLearning-SCU/2023-ICCV-COMMON.
summary: In this paper, we study a novel and widely existing problem in graph matching (GM), namely, Bi-level Noisy Correspondence (BNC), which refers to node-level noisy correspondence (NNC) and edge-level noisy correspondence (ENC). In brief, on the one hand, due to the poor recognizability and viewpoint differences between images, it is inevitable to inaccurately annotate some keypoints with offset and confusion, leading to the mismatch between two associated nodes, i.e., NNC. On the other hand, the noisy node-to-node correspondence will further contaminate the edge-to-edge correspondence, thus leading to ENC. For the BNC challenge, we propose a novel method termed Contrastive Matching with Momentum Distillation. Specifically, the proposed method is with a robust quadratic contrastive loss which enjoys the following merits: i) better exploring the node-to-node and edge-to-edge correlations through a GM customized quadratic contrastive learning paradigm; ii) adaptively penalizing the noisy assignments based on the confidence estimated by the momentum teacher. Extensive experiments on three real-world datasets show the robustness of our model compared with 12 competitive baselines. The code is available at https://github.com/XLearning-SCU/2023-ICCV-COMMON.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们研究了图匹配（GM）中一个新颖且广泛存在的问题，即双层噪声对应（BNC），它指的是节点级噪声对应（NNC）和边缘级噪声对应（ENC）。简而言之，一方面，由于图像之间的可识别性差和视点差异，不可避免地会出现一些关键点标注不准确、偏移和混乱的情况，导致两个关联节点（即NNC）之间的不匹配。另一方面，嘈杂的节点到节点的对应关系会进一步污染边到边的对应关系，从而导致ENC。对于 BNC 挑战，我们提出了一种称为动量蒸馏对比匹配的新方法。具体来说，所提出的方法具有鲁棒的二次对比损失，具有以下优点：i）通过GM定制的二次对比学习范式更好地探索节点到节点和边到边的相关性； ii）根据动量教师估计的置信度自适应地惩罚噪声作业。对三个真实世界数据集的大量实验表明，与 12 个竞争基线相比，我们的模型具有稳健性。代码可在 https://github.com/XLearning-SCU/2023-ICCV-COMMON 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文研究图匹配中的双层级噪声对应问题，提出了一种鲁棒的对比学习方法以处理节点级和边级噪声。
AI.motivation: 现有图匹配方法难以有效处理图像识别中的节点级噪声（NNC）和由此引发的边级噪声（ENC），导致匹配结果不准确。
AI.method: 提出对比蒸馏匹配方法（Contrastive Matching with Momentum Distillation），通过定制化二次对比损失函数，结合动量教师模型动态惩罚噪声分配。
AI.result: 在三个真实数据集上实验表明，该方法相比12种基线方法具有更强的鲁棒性，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过建模双层级噪声关联并引入动量蒸馏机制，有效提升了图匹配在噪声环境下的性能。

ai_content.result: 在三个真实数据集上实验表明，该方法相比12种基线方法具有更强的鲁棒性，代码已开源。
ai_content.conclusion: 所提方法通过建模双层级噪声关联并引入动量蒸馏机制，有效提升了图匹配在噪声环境下的性能。

tldr: 本文研究图匹配中的双层级噪声对应问题，提出了一种鲁棒的对比学习方法以处理节点级和边级噪声。
motivation: 现有图匹配方法难以有效处理图像识别中的节点级噪声（NNC）和由此引发的边级噪声（ENC），导致匹配结果不准确。

search_text: Graph Matching with Bi-level Noisy Correspondence 具有双层噪声对应的图匹配 In this paper, we study a novel and widely existing problem in graph matching (GM), namely, Bi-level Noisy Correspondence (BNC), which refers to node-level noisy correspondence (NNC) and edge-level noisy correspondence (ENC). In brief, on the one hand, due to the poor recognizability and viewpoint differences between images, it is inevitable to inaccurately annotate some keypoints with offset and confusion, leading to the mismatch between two associated nodes, i.e., NNC. On the other hand, the noisy node-to-node correspondence will further contaminate the edge-to-edge correspondence, thus leading to ENC. For the BNC challenge, we propose a novel method termed Contrastive Matching with Momentum Distillation. Specifically, the proposed method is with a robust quadratic contrastive loss which enjoys the following merits: i) better exploring the node-to-node and edge-to-edge correlations through a GM customized quadratic contrastive learning paradigm; ii) adaptively penalizing the noisy assignments based on the confidence estimated by the momentum teacher. Extensive experiments on three real-world datasets show the robustness of our model compared with 12 competitive baselines. The code is available at https://github.com/XLearning-SCU/2023-ICCV-COMMON. 在本文中，我们研究了图匹配（GM）中一个新颖且广泛存在的问题，即双层噪声对应（BNC），它指的是节点级噪声对应（NNC）和边缘级噪声对应（ENC）。简而言之，一方面，由于图像之间的可识别性差和视点差异，不可避免地会出现一些关键点标注不准确、偏移和混乱的情况，导致两个关联节点（即NNC）之间的不匹配。另一方面，嘈杂的节点到节点的对应关系会进一步污染边到边的对应关系，从而导致ENC。对于 BNC 挑战，我们提出了一种称为动量蒸馏对比匹配的新方法。具体来说，所提出的方法具有鲁棒的二次对比损失，具有以下优点：i）通过GM定制的二次对比学习范式更好地探索节点到节点和边到边的相关性； ii）根据动量教师估计的置信度自适应地惩罚噪声作业。对三个真实世界数据集的大量实验表明，与 12 个竞争基线相比，我们的模型具有稳健性。代码可在 https://github.com/XLearning-SCU/2023-ICCV-COMMON 获取。 本文研究图匹配中的双层级噪声对应问题，提出了一种鲁棒的对比学习方法以处理节点级和边级噪声。 Yijie Lin,Mouxing Yang,Jun Yu,Peng Hu,Changqing Zhang,Xi Peng cs.CV
